factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Shift
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair on May 22, described as 'most hawkish Fed nominee in 20 years' (Bloomberg). Markets pricing in a potential Fed rate hike by year-end per FXStreet, reversing prior expectations of cuts. Higher rates compress real yields.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Dollar recovering ground as Iran peace deal hopes fade and Fed hawkish tilt supports carry. Stronger USD is inversely correlated with gold; DXY recovery directly pressures XAUUSD.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Nominal 10-year Treasury yields hitting 20-year highs per Bloomberg ('Global Long Bond Yields Hit Highest in Almost Two Decades'). If real yields are rising alongside inflation uncertainty, this is bearish for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Appetite
factors.summary: US-Iran negotiations showing 'slight progress' with reported agreement on nuclear outlines (Sky News Arabia); Strait of Hormuz tensions easing reduces immediate crisis premium. Risk-off flow into gold weakening as safe-haven demand subsides.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Crude oil reaching 'new lows' per Sky News Arabia as geopolitical tensions ease and Iran talks progress. Lower oil reduces inflation expectations, supporting hawkish Fed and real yield rise, pressuring gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment Regime
factors.summary: Equities rallying ('Dow hits record high' per MarketWatch), but retreating from session highs as peace deal uncertainties remain. Mixed sentiment limits safe-haven demand for gold but not yet pushing pure risk-on.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment / Economic Softness
factors.summary: Consumer sentiment at all-time low per MarketWatch; Walmart executive departures suggest internal stress. Weak demand backdrop could ultimately support dovish pivot, but not yet priced in.

factors.name: Gold Price Action (4h & 24h)
factors.summary: Down 0.21% in 4h and 0.39% in 24h; move small but directionally consistent with hawkish Fed surprise. FXStreet notes gold 'edges lower' as Greenback recovers on Waller hawkish comments and Fed rate hike expectations.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying Structural Bid
factors.summary: No new CBG news in latest flow. Ongoing structural demand from PBoC, RBI remains but insufficient to offset near-term macro headwinds.

factors.name: Inflation Print Timing
factors.summary: No major CPI/PCE release in news window. Next data will be critical for real-yield trajectory; absence leaves market guessing on rate-hike probability.

screening.narrative: Kevin Warsh's appointment as Fed Chair on May 22 and characterization as the most hawkish nominee in 20 years has triggered a repricing of rate expectations, with markets now pricing in a potential hike by year-end. This sharp hawkish pivot, combined with fading geopolitical premium (US-Iran talks showing progress, crude oil breaking lower), has compressed safe-haven demand and pressured the dollar higher. Real yields are rising even as headline inflation risks ease, creating a headwind for gold. The 0.39% 24-hour decline and retest lower suggests Phase 2 (move in progress), not yet extended, but momentum is decidedly bearish into the weekend.
catalysts_ahead: Warsh's first FOMC meeting and forward guidance (TBD, likely June)
Next CPI/PCE release (likely early June) — inflation surprise could flip bias sharply
Next NFP (early June) — soft jobs data could create dovish pivot opportunity
Iran negotiations outcome (24-72h window per Sky News) — if deal announced, crude lower and risk-on could extend gold downside
Weekly jobless claims (Thursday) — early labor market signal
Trump policy announcements on tariffs/trade war — could reignite inflation fears and support gold
screening.risk_warning: If Iran nuclear talks collapse or Hormuz blockade escalates, safe-haven flows reverse and gold bounces sharply higher ($4,550+ in 24h). If next inflation print comes in hot (>consensus), real yields spike and Fed hawkishness inverts to demand destruction narrative, also supporting gold. Additionally, if US equity market rolls over and VIX spikes, gold's safe-haven bid could re-engage despite Fed tightness.
screening.entry_guidance: Sell bias is early-stage (Phase 2); avoid chasing lower into weekend with low liquidity. Better entry would be on a retest of $4,530–$4,545 resistance or a failed bounce, confirming breakdown. If price breaks below $4,480 support decisively, stop loss above $4,550 on short.
